title: Pradakshina (Circumambulation)
summary: Walking around the deity, temple, or sacred object in a clockwise direction. A universal Hindu practice, performed with specific counts per deity.

steps.title: Always walk clockwise
steps.instruction: Keep the deity on your right as you walk. Move slowly and mindfully — pradakshina is meditation in motion, not exercise.

steps.title: Count by the deity
steps.instruction: Commonly prescribed counts: Ganesha — 1, Surya — 2, Shiva — 3 (and the Shiva pradakshina goes only as far as the somasutra / drainage channel before turning back; one does not cross it), Vishnu — 4, Devi / Ambika — 4, Ashwattha (peepal) tree — 7 or 108. Where exact counts are unknown, 3 is the common default.

steps.title: Hold the right posture
steps.instruction: Fold hands at the chest. Keep eyes down or softly on the deity. Walk barefoot inside the temple.

steps.title: Close with namaskara
steps.instruction: Stop before the deity after the last round and offer a full or half prostration (sashtanga or panchanga namaskara).
